html:
Advanced MDX Features Demonstration

This document showcases unusual and advanced MDX features including ~~strikethrough~~, inline code, and <sup>superscript</sup> as well as <sub>subscript</sub> text.

Text Formatting Features

Emphasis and Strong Text

  • Bold text with code inside
  • Italic text with ~~strikethrough~~ mixed in
  • Bold italic combined formatting
  • Code with **bold** and *italic* inside

Special Characters and Escaping

  • HTML entities: & < > " '
  • Unicode: 🚀 💻 ⚡ 🎯
  • RTL text: مرحبا بالعالم
  • Math symbols: α β γ δ ε

Advanced Lists

Task Lists

  • [x] Completed task with bold text
  • [ ] Pending task with code
  • [ ] Another task with ~~strikethrough~~

Definition Lists

<dl> <dt>**Bold Term**</dt> <dd>Definition with *italic* and `code`</dd> <dt>`CodeTerm`</dt> <dd>Definition with ~~strikethrough~~ and <sup>superscript</sup></dd> </dl>

Nested Lists

  1. First level
    • Second level with bold
      • Third level with code
        • Fourth level with ~~strikethrough~~
  2. Back to first level
    • Mixed formatting: bold, italic, code, ~~strikethrough~~

Advanced Tables

| Feature | Description | Example | Status | |:--------|:-----------:|--------:|:------:| | Bold | Italic | Code | ✅ | | ~~Strikethrough~~ | <sup>Superscript</sup> | <sub>Subscript</sub> | ❌ | | Mixed: bold and italic | Code with **bold** | ~~Strike with code~~ | ⚠️ |

Complex Table with Nested Content

| Component | Props | Children | Notes | |-----------|-------|----------|-------| | <div> | CSS classes | Text content | Basic HTML | | <span> | Inline styles | Any content | Inline elements | | <p> | Text content | None | Paragraphs |

Code Blocks

JavaScript with Syntax Highlighting

// Simple function with template literals
const processData = (data, options = {}) => {
  const { 
    transform = (x) => x, 
    filter = () => true,
    sort = (a, b) => a - b 
  } = options;
  
  return data
    .filter(filter)
    .map(transform)
    .sort(sort);
};

// Usage with simple props
const result = processData(users, {
  transform: (user) => ({
    ...user,
    fullName: `${user.firstName} ${user.lastName}`
  }),
  filter: (user) => user.active,
  sort: (a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name)
});

TypeScript with Generics

interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  status: 'success' | 'error';
  message?: string;
}

class ApiClient<T> {
  constructor(private baseUrl: string) {}
  
  async fetch<R = T>(endpoint: string): Promise<ApiResponse<R>> {
    const response = await fetch(`${this.baseUrl}${endpoint}`);
    return response.json();
  }
}

// Usage
const userClient = new ApiClient<User>('/api/users');
const users = await userClient.fetch<User[]>('/list');

CSS with Custom Properties

:root {
  --primary-color: #007bff;
  --secondary-color: #6c757d;
  --success-color: #28a745;
  --danger-color: #dc3545;
}

.simple-component {
  --component-padding: 1rem;
  --component-border-radius: 0.5rem;
  
  padding: var(--component-padding);
  border-radius: var(--component-border-radius);
  background: linear-gradient(
    135deg,
    var(--primary-color),
    var(--secondary-color)
  );
  color: white;
  font-weight: bold;
  transition: all 0.3s ease;
}

.simple-component:hover {
  transform: translateY(-2px);
  box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(0, 123, 255, 0.3);
}

Math Expressions

Inline Math

The quadratic formula is: $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

Block Math

$$ \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi} $$

Complex Math

$$ \begin{align} \nabla \cdot \vec{E} &= \frac{\rho}{\epsilon_0} \\ \nabla \cdot \vec{B} &= 0 \\ \nabla \times \vec{E} &= -\frac{\partial \vec{B}}{\partial t} \\ \nabla \times \vec{B} &= \mu_0\vec{J} + \mu_0\epsilon_0\frac{\partial \vec{E}}{\partial t} \end{align} $$

Simple JSX Components

Basic Component

<div className="simple-component"> <h3>**Simple Component** with *mixed formatting*</h3> <p>This component includes:</p> <ul> <li>**Basic HTML** elements</li> <li>*Mixed formatting* in content</li> <li>`Code snippets` and ~~strikethrough~~</li> <li><sup>Superscript</sup> and <sub>subscript</sub></li> </ul> </div>

Fragment with Simple Content

<> <div className="conditional-content"> <h4>**Fragment content** with *formatting*</h4> <p>This content uses React fragments.</p> </div> </>

Blockquotes and Citations

This is a blockquote with mixed formatting and code.

It can span multiple lines and include:

  • Lists
  • Bold text
  • Italic text
  • Code snippets
  • ~~Strikethrough text~~

Nested blockquotes are also supported

With complex formatting and code

<blockquote cite="https://example.com"> <p>Blockquote with citation and <strong>HTML</strong> elements.</p> <footer>— <cite>Author Name</cite></footer> </blockquote>

Footnotes

Here's a sentence with a footnote[^1] and another one[^2].

[^1]: This is the first footnote with bold and italic text. [^2]: This is the second footnote with code and ~~strikethrough~~.

Comments

{/* This is a JSX comment that should be handled properly */} {/* Multi-line JSX comment with **bold** and *italic* text and `code` snippets */} {/* Another comment with special characters: & < > " ' */}

Advanced Links and References

Complex Links

  • Link with bold text
  • Link with code text
  • Link with ~~strikethrough~~

Reference Links

Complex reference with formatting.

Inline HTML

<div style={{ background: 'linear-gradient(45deg, #ff6b6b, #4ecdc4)', padding: '20px', borderRadius: '8px', color: 'white', margin: '20px 0' }}> <h4>**Inline HTML** with React styles</h4> <p>This div uses inline styles with:</p> <ul> <li>*Gradient backgrounds*</li> <li>`Custom properties`</li> <li>~~Complex styling~~</li> </ul> </div>

Simple Event Handlers

<div onClick={() => alert('Clicked!')} style={{ cursor: 'pointer', padding: '10px', border: '2px solid #007bff', borderRadius: '4px', backgroundColor: '#f8f9fa' }} > <p>**Clickable div** with event handlers</p> <p>This demonstrates basic interactivity.</p> </div>

Accessibility Features

<div role="main" aria-label="Main content area"> <section aria-labelledby="section-title"> <h2 id="section-title">**Accessible Section**</h2> <p>This section includes proper ARIA attributes and semantic HTML.</p> <button aria-describedby="button-description" onClick={() => console.log('Accessible button clicked')} > Accessible Button </button> <div id="button-description" aria-hidden="true"> This button performs an accessible action </div> </section> </div>

Simple Expressions

<div> <p>**Dynamic content** examples:</p> <ul> <li>Simple expression: {2 + 2} = 4</li> <li>String template: {`Hello ${'World'}`}</li> <li>Boolean: {true ? 'Yes' : 'No'}</li> </ul> </div>

Spread Operators

<div {...{ className: "spread-example", "data-testid": "spread-component" }} style={{ '--custom-property': 'value', '--another-property': 'another-value' }} > <p>This div uses **spread operators** and *complex props*.</p> <p>It demonstrates advanced React patterns with `template literals`.</p> </div>

This document demonstrates comprehensive MDX features including ~~advanced~~ formatting, code blocks, and <sup>complex</sup> <sub>structures</sub>.
